---
title: "TensorTemporalNeighbors"
---

## Overview

`TensorTemporalNeighbors` is a TransformsV2 container transform (TensorData → TensorData) that
augments a time-indexed tensor with feature columns from neighboring time indices. This enables
lag/lead feature engineering for downstream models such as Hidden Markov Models, without manual
alignment code.

## Location

- Header: `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorTemporalNeighbors/TensorTemporalNeighbors.hpp`
- Implementation: `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorTemporalNeighbors/TensorTemporalNeighbors.cpp`
- Tests: `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorTemporalNeighbors/TensorTemporalNeighbors.test.cpp`

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `offsets` | `std::vector<int>` | (required) | Integer offsets relative to each row in sorted time order (e.g. `{-1, +1}` for previous/next row) |
| `boundary_policy` | `BoundaryPolicy` | `NaN` | How to handle rows where an offset falls outside the tensor: `NaN`, `Drop`, `Clamp`, or `Zero` |
| `column_indices` | `std::optional<std::vector<std::size_t>>` | all | Subset of source column indices to shift |
| `include_original` | `bool` | `true` | Whether to include the original (unshifted) columns in the output |

## Time-Alignment Rule

Neighbors are determined by **sorted row position** in `TimeIndexStorage`, not by raw frame number ±1.
For example, if rows exist at frames `[10, 20, 50]`, offset `+1` from frame 10 yields the row at
frame 20 (the next stored row), not frame 11 (which has no data).

Both `DenseTimeIndexStorage` and `SparseTimeIndexStorage` guarantee sorted indices.

## Boundary Policies

| Policy | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `NaN` | Fill missing neighbor features with `NaN`. All rows preserved. |
| `Drop` | Remove rows where any offset is out-of-range. Output has fewer rows. |
| `Clamp` | Clamp to first/last row when an offset goes out-of-range. |
| `Zero` | Fill missing neighbor features with `0.0f`. |

## Output Layout

Columns are ordered as: `[original cols] [offset_1 cols] [offset_2 cols] ...`

Shifted columns are named `"{original_col}_lag{offset}"`, e.g. `feat1_lag-1`, `feat1_lag+1`.

If `include_original` is `false`, original columns are omitted.

## Constraints

- Input must be 2D
- Input must have `RowType::TimeFrameIndex` (Interval and Ordinal rows are rejected)
- Returns `nullptr` on validation failure

## Pipeline JSON Example

```json
{
  "transformations": [
    {
      "transform_name": "TensorTemporalNeighbors",
      "input_key": "features",
      "output_key": "augmented_features",
      "params": {
        "offsets": [-1, 1],
        "boundary_policy": "NaN",
        "include_original": true
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Registration

Registered as `"TensorTemporalNeighbors"` in `RegisteredTransforms.cpp` under category
**Feature Engineering**.
